title: Launching Health in ChatGPT
confirmed_fact: OpenAI says Health in ChatGPT is rolling out to logged-in Free, Go, Plus, and Pro users in the United States who are 18 or older, on web and iOS, and can connect supported health records and Apple Health data.
interpretation: This is a concrete sign that ChatGPT is being used for bounded, higher-stakes personal workflows rather than only open-ended chat.

title: Google introduces new AI labels for Ads
confirmed_fact: Google says it is adding a 'How this ad was made' panel in My Ad Center across Search, YouTube, and Discover, and that ads made with Google’s own generative AI tools will be automatically disclosed.
interpretation: AI transparency is becoming a normal product requirement, not an afterthought, and ad buyers should expect disclosure rules to keep tightening.

title: Introducing Canva AI 2.0: Reimagining how the world creates
confirmed_fact: Canva says Canva AI 2.0 is available today as a research preview and adds conversational design, iterative editing, layered object intelligence, living memory, and new workflows including connectors, scheduling, web research, brand intelligence, Sheets AI, and Canva Code 2.0.
interpretation: Canva is pushing beyond one-shot generation and toward a broader creation workspace that may matter more to teams than a standalone image tool.

title: Introducing a way to reflect on how you use Claude
confirmed_fact: Anthropic says the Reflect feature is in beta for Free, Pro, and Max users with memory turned on, and it lets users review past usage patterns, set quiet hours, and see prompts about how they want to use AI.
interpretation: The product is moving from pure generation toward self-management, which is useful for people trying to control AI habits and spending time more deliberately.
